facts.text: A royal flush (A-K-Q-J-10 of the same suit) is the highest-ranking hand in Texas Hold'em and occurs roughly once every 649,740 hands.
The World Series of Poker (WSOP) is held annually in Las Vegas and is the most prestigious poker tournament series in the world.
Chris Moneymaker won the 2003 WSOP Main Event after qualifying through a $39 online satellite on PokerStars.
In Texas Hold'em, each player receives two hole cards face down, and five community cards are dealt face up on the board.
Phil Hellmuth holds the record for the most WSOP bracelets, having won 17 over his career.
The 'dead man's hand'—aces and eights—is named after the hand Wild Bill Hickok was allegedly holding when he was shot dead in 1876.
Pot odds compare the current size of the pot to the cost of a contemplated call, helping players decide whether a call is mathematically profitable.
Omaha Hold'em requires players to use exactly two of their four hole cards combined with exactly three of the five community cards.
The hole-card camera, invented by Henry Orenstein in the late 1990s, revolutionized televised poker by allowing viewers to see players' hidden cards.
Doyle Brunson won back-to-back WSOP Main Events in 1976 and 1977, both times with a final hand of 10-2.
In No-Limit Texas Hold'em, a player can bet any amount up to and including all of their chips at any time.
The term 'bluff' in poker refers to betting or raising with a hand that is not believed to be the best, hoping opponents will fold superior hands.
PokerStars is the world's largest online poker site, having dealt its 200 billionth hand in 2019.
Phil Ivey is widely considered one of the greatest poker players of all time, with 10 WSOP bracelets and millions in tournament earnings.
The small blind is typically half the size of the big blind, and both are forced bets posted before any cards are dealt.
Black Friday in online poker refers to April 15, 2011, when the U.S. Department of Justice shut down PokerStars, Full Tilt Poker, and Absolute Poker.
GTO (Game Theory Optimal) play is a strategy where a player makes decisions that cannot be exploited, regardless of what the opponent does.
Seven-Card Stud was the most popular form of poker in American casinos before Texas Hold'em overtook it in the early 2000s.
The World Poker Tour (WPT) debuted on the Travel Channel in 2003 and helped popularize televised poker.
Daniel Negreanu, known as 'Kid Poker,' has won six WSOP bracelets and two WPT titles, accumulating over $42 million in live tournament earnings.
A 'tell' in poker is a change in a player's behavior or demeanor that gives clues about the strength of their hand.
Poker originated in the early 19th century in New Orleans, likely evolving from the French game 'poque' and the German game 'pochen.'
In tournament poker, players buy in for a fixed amount and receive a set number of chips; play continues until one player has all the chips.
The 'Moneymaker Effect' refers to the massive poker boom that followed Chris Moneymaker's 2003 WSOP Main Event victory, which proved an amateur could win.
Position is one of the most important concepts in poker—players who act later in a betting round have more information and a strategic advantage.
Razz is a form of stud poker where the lowest hand wins, with the best possible hand being A-2-3-4-5.
The rake is the commission taken by a casino or cardroom for hosting a poker game, typically a small percentage of each pot.
Vanessa Selbst is one of the most successful female poker players in history, with three WSOP bracelets and over $11 million in career tournament earnings.
Implied odds extend pot odds by estimating how much more money a player can win on future streets if they hit their draw.
The first WSOP Main Event was held in 1970 at Binion's Horseshoe casino in Las Vegas, with Johnny Moss voted the winner by his peers.
HORSE is a mixed poker game that rotates through five variants: Hold'em, Omaha Hi-Lo, Razz, Seven-Card Stud, and Seven-Card Stud Eight-or-Better.
In Texas Hold'em, the three community cards dealt after the first round of betting are called the 'flop.'
Phil Hellmuth is known as the 'Poker Brat' for his animated reactions and frequent outbursts at the table.
Poker was played on Mississippi riverboats in the 1800s, where professional gamblers would travel up and down the river seeking games.
Short Deck Hold'em (also called Six Plus Hold'em) removes all cards below six from the deck, creating a faster-paced game with more action.
The probability of being dealt pocket aces (AA) in Texas Hold'em is approximately 1 in 221, or about 0.45%.
Bryn Kenney held the record for the most all-time live tournament earnings at over $56 million before being surpassed.
A continuation bet (c-bet) is a bet made on the flop by the player who was the pre-flop aggressor, regardless of whether the flop improved their hand.
High Stakes Poker was a popular cash game television show that aired on the Game Show Network from 2006 to 2011.
Stu Ungar won the WSOP Main Event three times (1980, 1981, and 1997) and is considered one of the greatest natural card players ever.
Expected value (EV) in poker is a mathematical calculation that represents the average amount a player expects to win or lose on a decision over the long run.
In Pot-Limit Omaha (PLO), the maximum bet a player can make is the size of the current pot.
The 'button' or 'dealer button' rotates clockwise around the table, indicating which player is the nominal dealer for that hand.
Chris Moneymaker's WSOP Main Event win in 2003 turned a $39 satellite buy-in into a $2.5 million first prize.
Poker After Dark was an NBC television show that featured high-stakes cash games and sit-and-go tournaments with top professional players.
The 'nuts' in poker refers to the best possible hand given the community cards on the board.
Early poker was played with a 20-card deck (A-K-Q-J-10) among four players, with each receiving five cards.
Justin Bonomo, known as 'ZeeJustin,' has accumulated over $57 million in live tournament earnings throughout his career.
A 'bad beat' occurs when a player with a strong hand loses to an opponent who had a statistically unlikely draw.
Mike Caro's 'Book of Tells' is one of the most influential poker books ever written, cataloging physical behaviors that reveal hand strength.
In tournament poker, blinds and antes increase at regular intervals to force action and prevent overly conservative play.
The Bellagio poker room in Las Vegas is one of the most famous cardrooms in the world, hosting high-stakes cash games.
Exploitative play in poker involves adjusting strategy to take advantage of specific weaknesses in opponents' games, as opposed to playing GTO.
Poker was officially recognized by the International Mind Sports Association in 2010.
A 'satellite' tournament is a lower buy-in event where the prize is entry into a larger, more expensive tournament.
The 52-card deck became standard for poker around the 1830s, replacing the original 20-card deck and allowing for more players and hand combinations.
Chinese Poker is a variant where four players each receive 13 cards and must arrange them into three separate hands: front, middle, and back.
In a cash game, players can typically buy in for any amount between the table minimum and maximum, and chips represent real money.
The 'river' is the fifth and final community card dealt in Texas Hold'em and Omaha, named because riverboat gamblers would deal it by the river.
Tom Dwan, known as 'durrrr,' became famous for playing the highest online poker stakes and his aggressive style.
Pot odds are expressed as a ratio—if the pot is $100 and you must call $20, your pot odds are 5:1.
The Commerce Casino in Los Angeles has one of the largest poker rooms in the world, with over 200 tables.
A 'check-raise' is a deceptive play where a player first checks, then raises after an opponent bets.
The WSOP Main Event buy-in has been $10,000 since 1972, making it one of the most expensive and prestigious poker tournaments.
Poker hand rankings from highest to lowest are: royal flush, straight flush, four of a kind, full house, flush, straight, three of a kind, two pair, one pair, high card.
Johnny Chan won back-to-back WSOP Main Events in 1987 and 1988, earning the nickname 'Orient Express.'
In poker, 'tilt' refers to a state of emotional frustration that causes a player to make suboptimal decisions.
GGPoker has become one of the largest online poker platforms, hosting the WSOP Online bracelet events since 2020.
The 'turn' is the fourth community card dealt in Texas Hold'em and Omaha, coming after the flop.
Wild West saloons were notorious for poker games, and disputes over hands sometimes ended in gunfights.
A 'value bet' is made when a player believes they have the best hand and bets an amount designed to be called by a weaker hand.
Fedor Holz retired from poker at age 23 after winning over $32 million in live tournaments, then later returned to the game.
The 'Cadillac of Poker' is a nickname for No-Limit Texas Hold'em, coined by Doyle Brunson in his book 'Super/System.'
In heads-up poker, only two players compete, and the small blind is on the button and acts first pre-flop but last on subsequent streets.
Nevada legalized poker and other forms of gambling in 1931, paving the way for Las Vegas to become the poker capital of the world.
A 'semi-bluff' is a bet or raise with a hand that is not currently the best but has the potential to improve on future streets.
The WSOP introduced online bracelet events in 2015, allowing players to compete for official bracelets from their computers.
Pocket kings (KK) are the second-best starting hand in Texas Hold'em, behind only pocket aces (AA).
Doyle Brunson's 'Super/System,' published in 1978, was the first major poker strategy book and is often called the 'Bible of Poker.'
A 'float play' is calling a bet on one street with the intention of bluffing on a later street when the opponent shows weakness.
The 2006 WSOP Main Event attracted a record 8,773 entries, the largest poker tournament field at the time.
In Seven-Card Stud, there are no community cards—each player receives seven cards (three face down and four face up) and makes the best five-card hand.
Erik Seidel has won nine WSOP bracelets and appeared at numerous WPT final tables over a career spanning four decades.
Phil Ivey's nickname is 'The Tiger Woods of Poker' for his dominance across multiple poker variants.
In poker, 'range' refers to the complete set of hands a player could have in a given situation, based on their actions.
The Poker Hall of Fame, established in 1979, honors the game's most influential players and contributors.
A 'donk bet' is a bet made by a player out of position who was not the aggressor on the previous street, often considered unorthodox.
The 2003 WSOP Main Event had 839 entries, up from 631 the year before, largely due to the growing popularity of online poker.
Pocket aces (AA) are statistically the best starting hand in Texas Hold'em, winning approximately 85% of the time heads-up pre-flop.
Online poker first appeared in the late 1990s, with Planet Poker dealing the first real-money online hand on January 1, 1998.
A 'squeeze play' is a pre-flop re-raise made after an initial raise and one or more callers, designed to pick up the pot without a fight.
The 'poker face' is the ability to maintain a neutral expression to prevent opponents from reading one's emotions or hand strength.
Las Vegas's 'Poker Row' on the Strip features poker rooms at the Bellagio, Aria, Wynn, and Venetian.
A 'cooler' in poker is a situation where a strong hand loses to an even stronger hand, and neither player could realistically fold.
Jamie Gold won the 2006 WSOP Main Event, taking home a record $12 million first prize.
In poker, 'fold equity' is the probability that an opponent will fold to a bet or raise, giving value to an aggressive action.
Antonio Esfandiari won the $1 million buy-in Big One for One Drop tournament in 2012, earning $18.3 million—the largest single tournament prize in poker history at the time.
The 'dealer's choice' format allows the player in the dealer position to select which poker variant will be played for that hand or round.
Ace-King suited is considered one of the strongest starting hands in Texas Hold'em, nicknamed 'Big Slick.'
Scotty Nguyen won the 1998 WSOP Main Event, famously saying 'You call, it's gonna be all over, baby' on the final hand.
A 'three-bet' (3-bet) is the third bet in a sequence—the big blind is the first bet, a raise is the second, and a re-raise is the third.
Daniel Negreanu's ability to read opponents and put them on specific hands has earned him the reputation as one of the best hand readers in poker.
In poker, 'ICM' (Independent Chip Model) is a mathematical model used to calculate a player's equity in a tournament based on their chip stack.
The 'bubble' in a poker tournament is the point just before the remaining players reach the money, creating maximum pressure.
Patrik Antonius is a Finnish poker professional known for playing the highest-stakes cash games both live and online.
A 'limp' in poker means calling the big blind pre-flop rather than raising, often considered a passive play.
The WSOP Player of the Year award has been given annually since 2004, recognizing consistent performance across multiple WSOP events.
The odds of hitting a flush draw (four cards to a flush) on the turn are approximately 19.1%, or about 4.2:1 against.
Gus Hansen, the 'Great Dane,' is known for his ultra-aggressive style and won three WPT titles.
A 'straddle' is an optional blind bet made before the cards are dealt, typically double the big blind, that gives the straddler last action pre-flop.
The Commerce Casino's poker room operates 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, offering games at every stake level.
In draw poker, players can discard and replace cards from their hand to try to improve it.
Phil Hellmuth won his first WSOP Main Event bracelet in 1989 at age 24, the youngest champion at the time.
A 'nit' in poker slang refers to an extremely tight player who only plays premium hands.
The WSOP Circuit is a series of regional tournament events held across the United States, offering gold rings instead of bracelets.
Poker solvers are computer programs that calculate GTO strategies for specific poker situations, revolutionizing how top players study the game.
In Omaha Hi-Lo, the pot is split between the best high hand and the best qualifying low hand (eight or lower).
Barry Greenstein, known as the 'Robin Hood of Poker,' donates his tournament winnings to charity.
A 'gutshot' (or inside straight draw) has only four outs to complete a straight, with roughly an 8.5% chance of hitting on the next card.
The WSOP moved from Binion's Horseshoe to the Rio All-Suite Hotel and Casino in 2005.
In poker, 'running it twice' means dealing the remaining community cards two separate times and splitting the pot based on both outcomes.
Jennifer Harman is one of the most accomplished female cash game players, known for competing in the biggest games at the Bellagio.
A 'snap call' is an immediate call made without hesitation, usually indicating a very strong hand.
The 'one-chip rule' in live poker states that placing a single chip in the pot without announcing a raise is considered a call.
The probability of flopping a set (three of a kind) when holding a pocket pair is approximately 11.8%, or about 7.5:1 against.
Isai Scheinberg founded PokerStars in 2001, which grew to become the dominant force in online poker.
A 'stop-and-go' play involves calling a bet on one street and then leading out with a bet on the next street.
Mixed games, which rotate through multiple poker variants, are popular among skilled players who want to test their versatility.
The 'ante' is a forced bet that every player at the table must post before a hand begins, most common in stud games and late tournament stages.
Peter Eastgate became the youngest WSOP Main Event champion in 2008 at age 22, before that record was broken by Joe Cada in 2009.
In poker, 'blockers' are cards in a player's hand that reduce the likelihood of an opponent holding certain combinations.
Annette Obrestad won the 2007 World Series of Poker Europe Main Event at age 18, becoming the youngest bracelet winner.
A 'min-raise' is the smallest legal raise in a hand, equal to the size of the previous bet or raise.
The Venetian poker room in Las Vegas hosts the popular Deep Stack Extravaganza series every summer.
Open-ended straight draws have eight outs to complete the straight, giving approximately a 31.5% chance of hitting by the river.
Joe Hachem won the 2005 WSOP Main Event, becoming the first Australian to claim poker's most coveted title.
Limit Hold'em restricts the size of bets and raises to predetermined amounts, unlike No-Limit where players can bet any amount.
The 'Moneymaker Effect' led to a tripling of the 2004 WSOP Main Event field to 2,576 players from 839 the year before.
A 'tank' in poker refers to taking a long time to make a decision, often indicating a close or difficult situation.
Poker rooms in casinos typically offer both cash games and tournaments, each with different rules and strategies.
In Texas Hold'em, suited connectors (like 7♠8♠) can make both straights and flushes, giving them versatility.
Greg Raymer won the 2004 WSOP Main Event, earning $5 million and famously wearing his signature fossil-shaped card protector.
A 'rainbow' flop in Texas Hold'em means all three community cards are of different suits, eliminating flush draw possibilities on the flop.
Stu Ungar's tragic story—a brilliant player who struggled with addiction—is one of the most compelling narratives in poker history.
Table stakes is the rule in most modern poker games that a player can only bet the chips they have on the table at the start of a hand.
The 'gap concept' states that a player needs a stronger hand to call a raise than to make a raise themselves.
In poker, 'dead money' refers to money in the pot contributed by players who have already folded.
Robert Varkonyi's surprise victory in the 2002 WSOP Main Event featured him bluffing Phil Hellmuth off a hand with a stone-cold bluff.
A 'smooth call' (or flat call) is calling a bet with a strong hand instead of raising, often to disguise the hand's strength.
The Aria poker room in Las Vegas is known for hosting some of the biggest high-stakes cash games in the world.
In tournament poker, the 'chip leader' is the player with the most chips at any given point in the event.
Mike McDermott, played by Matt Damon in the 1998 film 'Rounders,' helped bring poker into mainstream pop culture.
A 'double belly buster' is two inside straight draws that together give eight outs, the same as an open-ended straight draw.
The 2005 WSOP Main Event set a record with 5,619 entries, more than doubling the previous year's field.
In poker, 'equity' refers to a player's share of the pot based on the probability of winning the hand.
Sam Farha, known for his unlit cigarette at the table, finished second to Chris Moneymaker in the 2003 WSOP Main Event.
A 'brick' is a community card that appears to help no one and is unlikely to change the dynamics of the hand.
The World Poker Tour introduced the concept of fixed camera angles and a final table format that made poker entertaining on television.
Pocket jacks (JJ) are often called 'hooks' and are considered one of the trickiest starting hands to play in Hold'em.
The Unlawful Internet Gambling Enforcement Act (UIGEA) was signed into law on October 13, 2006, restricting online gambling transactions in the U.S.
A 'wet' board in poker has many connected and suited cards, creating numerous draw possibilities.
Viktor 'Isildur1' Blom burst onto the online poker scene in 2009, playing nosebleed stakes and winning millions against top pros.
The 'early position' at a poker table refers to the seats immediately to the left of the big blind, which act first post-flop.
Deuce-to-seven (2-7) triple draw lowball is a draw poker variant where the goal is to make the lowest possible hand without straights or flushes.
In poker, a 'family pot' is a hand where all or nearly all players at the table see the flop.
Chris Ferguson, known as 'Jesus' for his long hair and beard, won the 2000 WSOP Main Event.
A 'dry' board has few connected or suited cards, making it unlikely that draws are present.
The PokerStars Caribbean Adventure (PCA) in the Bahamas is one of the most popular live poker stops outside of Las Vegas.
In poker, 'range merging' is betting a strong hand at a size typically used for bluffs, making it harder for opponents to read.
T.J. Cloutier is one of the most prolific tournament players ever, with six WSOP bracelets and over 100 major tournament wins.
The odds of being dealt any specific hand in Texas Hold'em (like A♠K♠) are 1 in 1,326.
The first World Series of Poker Europe (WSOPE) was held in London in September 2007.
A 'set' is three of a kind made with a pocket pair and one matching board card, while 'trips' uses one hole card and two board cards.
John Juanda has won five WSOP bracelets and is known for his consistent results across multiple poker disciplines.
In poker, 'barreling' refers to making continuation bets on multiple streets—a 'double barrel' is c-betting the flop and turn.
The Horseshoe Casino in downtown Las Vegas was the original home of the World Series of Poker from 1970 to 2004.
A 'chop' occurs when two or more players agree to divide the remaining prize money, usually near the end of a tournament.
Joe Cada became the youngest WSOP Main Event champion at age 21 in 2009.
In poker, 'reverse implied odds' account for money a player may lose on future streets if they hit their draw but still lose.
The term 'all-in' means committing all remaining chips to the pot, a defining moment in no-limit poker.
Poker etiquette includes not discussing the hand while it's in play, acting in turn, and not slow-rolling (deliberately delaying when revealing a winning hand).
The Wynn poker room is known for its high-end atmosphere and regularly spreads mid-to-high-stakes cash games.
In poker, 'over-cards' are cards in a player's hand that are higher than any card on the board.
The 2004 WSOP Main Event was won by Greg Raymer, a patent attorney who qualified online.
A 'shove' is poker slang for going all-in, often used in short-stacked tournament situations.
Phil Galfond, founder of the online poker site Run It Once, is considered one of the greatest PLO players ever.
In poker, a 'probe bet' is a bet made by an in-position player after the out-of-position player checks, typically when the pre-flop aggressor checks.
Ace-Queen is sometimes called 'Big Chick' or 'Little Slick' and is considered a tricky hand to play against pre-flop raises.
The 'standard raise' in No-Limit Hold'em is typically 2.5 to 3 times the big blind pre-flop.
Robert Turner invented HORSE, the mixed game format that became one of the most respected events at the WSOP.
In poker, a 'calling station' is a player who calls bets too frequently and rarely folds, making them difficult to bluff.
The 'cutoff' is the seat immediately to the right of the dealer button and is considered a strong position for stealing blinds.
A 'four-bet' (4-bet) pre-flop is typically a very strong play, usually representing premium hands like AA, KK, QQ, or AK.
The Poker Players Alliance (PPA) was a lobbying group that advocated for the legalization and regulation of online poker in the U.S.
In poker, 'polarized' ranges contain very strong hands and bluffs, with few medium-strength hands.
Ted Forrest once won a famous bet by losing over 40 pounds in two months while competing at the WSOP.
Pocket queens (QQ) are known as 'ladies' and are the third-best starting hand in Texas Hold'em.
The 'hijack' seat is two positions to the right of the button and offers late-position advantages with slightly more risk.
In poker, a 'bounty' tournament awards a cash prize to players for eliminating opponents.
Huck Seed won the 1996 WSOP Main Event, defeating Dr. Bruce Van Horn heads-up.
A 'deep stack' game features stacks of 200 or more big blinds, allowing for more complex post-flop play.
Matt Damon and Ben Affleck are both avid poker players, with Affleck winning a California State Poker Championship.
In poker, 'multi-tabling' refers to playing multiple tables simultaneously online, a practice that can increase hourly winrate.
The 'fold to 3-bet' statistic measures how often a player folds after raising and facing a re-raise pre-flop.
A 'string bet' is an illegal move in live poker where a player doesn't put in the full amount of a raise in one motion.
The European Poker Tour (EPT) was founded in 2004 and has held events in cities across Europe, including Monte Carlo, Barcelona, and Prague.
In poker, 'board texture' refers to how the community cards interact with each other—whether they're connected, suited, or paired.
Amarillo Slim won the 1972 WSOP Main Event and became the first poker player to appear on The Tonight Show with Johnny Carson.
A 'chop the blinds' agreement allows the small and big blind to take back their bets if all other players fold pre-flop in a cash game.
In poker, 'balance' means mixing different hands into the same action (e.g., raising with both strong hands and bluffs) to avoid being predictable.
The odds of two players both being dealt pocket aces in a full ring Hold'em game are approximately 1 in 270,725.
The Triton Poker Series features some of the highest buy-in events in the world, attracting the wealthiest players.
In poker, 'leveling' or 'level thinking' refers to thinking about what your opponent thinks you have, and what they think you think they have.
Doyle Brunson's famous 'Super/System' originally sold for $100 in 1978, a premium price that reflected the value of the strategy within.
A 'multiway pot' involves three or more players, requiring adjustments to strategy compared to heads-up play.
The 'under the gun' (UTG) position is the first player to act pre-flop, sitting immediately to the left of the big blind.
In poker, 'protection' is betting a strong but vulnerable hand to prevent opponents from seeing cheap cards that could beat it.
Martin Jacobson won the 2014 WSOP Main Event November Nine, taking home $10 million.
A 'blocker bet' is a small bet made to prevent an opponent from making a larger bet, controlling the pot size.
Poker is played in over 100 countries worldwide and every inhabited continent.
In poker, 'isolating' means raising to narrow the field and play heads-up against a weaker player.
The 'Rule of 2 and 4' is a quick method for estimating hand equity: multiply outs by 4 on the flop or by 2 on the turn.
Full Tilt Poker was one of the largest online poker sites until its collapse following Black Friday in 2011.
Scotty Nguyen won the 1998 WSOP $10,000 Limit Hold'em Championship and the Main Event in the same year, an extremely rare accomplishment.
In poker, 'opening range' is the set of hands with which a player will make the first voluntary action (raise or limp).
The 'November Nine' format was used by the WSOP Main Event from 2008 to 2016, pausing the final table until November.
A 'muck' is the pile of discarded cards, and 'mucking' means folding your hand without showing it.
In Stud poker, Third Street is when each player has three cards (two down, one up) and betting begins.
The 'coffeehousing' rule prohibits verbal misdirection or deceptive talk during a poker hand in many cardrooms.
Jack Binion and his father Benny Binion created the World Series of Poker to attract high-stakes gamblers to their casino.
A 'freeroll' in poker can mean a tournament with no entry fee, or a situation where a player can win but cannot lose.
Doug Polk used an aggressive heads-up strategy to win over $1.2 million from Daniel Negreanu in their 2020-2021 heads-up challenge.
In poker, a 'Sit & Go' (SNG) is a tournament that begins as soon as enough players register, typically with one or a few tables.
The 'pot-committed' concept means a player has invested so much of their stack that folding is no longer mathematically correct.
A 'rebuy' tournament allows players who lose their chips during a set period to buy back in for another stack.
The WSOP Europe moved from London to Rozvadov, Czech Republic, in 2017.
In poker, 'hand history' refers to the record of all actions taken during a hand, widely used for review and analysis in online play.
The WSOP bracelet is one of the most coveted awards in competitive gaming, made of gold and precious stones.
A 'turbo' tournament has faster blind levels than standard events, requiring more aggressive play and push-fold strategy.
In poker, 'stack-to-pot ratio' (SPR) helps determine optimal post-flop strategy based on the effective stack relative to the pot.
Brunson, Chip Reese, and other legends regularly played in the 'Big Game' at the Bellagio, one of poker's most famous cash games.
Jonathan Duhamel won the 2010 WSOP Main Event as the first Canadian champion, earning $8.9 million.
In poker, 'overbetting' means betting more than the current pot size, a strategy used to maximize fold equity or value.
The hole-card camera technology was first used on the UK's Late Night Poker show before being adopted in the United States.
A 'freezeout' tournament is the most traditional format—once a player loses all chips, they are eliminated with no rebuy option.
In poker, 'relative hand strength' evaluates a hand based on the board texture and opponents' likely ranges, not just absolute rankings.
Chip Reese won the inaugural $50,000 H.O.R.S.E. event at the 2006 WSOP, which was named in his honor after his death.
The largest online poker tournament in history had over 253,000 entries on PokerStars in 2020.
A 'read' in poker is a judgment about an opponent's likely holding based on their betting patterns, timing, and physical behavior.
In poker, 'metagame' refers to the strategies and adjustments players make based on history and table dynamics beyond the current hand.
The Bicycle Casino (The Bike) in Bell Gardens, California, is one of the oldest and largest card rooms in the United States.
Jerry Yang won the 2007 WSOP Main Event, earning $8.25 million from a field of 6,358 players.
In poker, 'deepstack' tournaments start with 200+ big blinds, allowing for more nuanced multi-street play.
Vanessa Rousso reached the WSOP Main Event final table and competed on The Amazing Race, bringing poker visibility to mainstream TV.
A 'walk' occurs when all players fold to the big blind pre-flop, giving the big blind the pot uncontested.
In poker solvers, 'node locking' allows a player to fix an opponent's strategy to find the best counter-strategy.
The 'poker economy' refers to the flow of money through the ecosystem—from recreational players to professionals and the house rake.
The first televised poker tournament on ESPN was the WSOP Main Event in 1973.
In poker, 'variance' describes the natural ups and downs of results due to luck, even when a player makes optimal decisions.
A 'limper' enters the pot by just calling the big blind without raising, and 'over-limping' means limping behind other limpers.
In No-Limit Hold'em, position-aware players typically open-raise from late position with a wider range than from early position.
The 'poker calculator' is software that computes the probability of each player winning based on known cards.
Peter Eastgate's victory in the 2008 WSOP Main Event earned him $9.15 million, the second-largest Main Event prize at the time.
A 'donkey' or 'fish' is poker slang for a weak or inexperienced player.
In 2023, Daniel Weinman won the WSOP Main Event's $10 million first prize from a field of 10,043 entries.
In poker, 'slow playing' (or trapping) means playing a strong hand passively to disguise its strength and induce bluffs.
The 'gap concept' by David Sklansky states you need a better hand to call a raise than to make one, due to fold equity.
In five-card draw poker, players receive five cards and can exchange up to three (or four with an ace) for new ones.
The 2019 WSOP Main Event champion Hossein Ensan won $10 million from a record 8,569-entry field.
A 'time bank' in tournament poker gives players a set amount of extra time for difficult decisions.
Women in Poker Hall of Fame was established to honor the contributions of women to the game of poker.
In poker, 'hand combinatorics' (or combos) counts the number of ways a specific hand can be made—for instance, there are 6 combos of any pocket pair.
The 'kicker' is the side card used to break ties between hands of the same rank; a higher kicker wins.
Poker tracking software like PokerTracker and Hold'em Manager collects and analyzes hand data to reveal patterns and leaks.
Brunson, Amarillo Slim, and Sailor Roberts drove across Texas together in the 1960s, playing in high-stakes games and hustling.
A 'one-outer' is the situation where a player needs one specific card to win—the most extreme form of a bad beat.
In pot-limit poker, the maximum raise is the total amount currently in the pot plus the amount needed to call.
The 'action' in poker refers to whose turn it is to act, or generally to the betting activity at a table.
Qui Nguyen won the 2016 WSOP Main Event, the last year the November Nine format was used.
In poker, 'table image' is the perception other players have of your style based on how you've played recent hands.
Poker chips were introduced in the mid-1800s to replace gold nuggets, coins, and other valuables used for wagering.
A 'post oak bluff' is a small, understated bet designed to look like a value bet but is actually a bluff.
In poker, 'effective stacks' refers to the smaller of two players' stacks in a heads-up situation, as that's the most either can win.
The WSOP introduced a dedicated Poker Hall of Fame ceremony during the series, honoring new inductees each year.
Jonathan Little is a poker coach, author, and two-time WPT champion who has built one of the largest poker training platforms.
In poker, a 'bomb pot' is a unique hand where every player puts in a set amount and the flop is dealt immediately with no pre-flop action.
The probability of completing a flush draw from flop to river (two cards to come) is approximately 35%.
A 'run good' or 'hot streak' in poker is a period of favorable results, which may be due to variance rather than skill.
Wild Bill Hickok was shot and killed while playing poker at Nuttal & Mann's Saloon in Deadwood, South Dakota, on August 2, 1876.
In poker, 'hand for hand' play occurs near the bubble, where each table must complete one hand before the next one is dealt.
Los Angeles County has the largest concentration of poker card rooms in the world.
David 'Devilfish' Ulliott was a colorful British poker player who won a WSOP bracelet and a WPT title before his death in 2015.
A 'satellite' into the WSOP Main Event typically costs between $100 and $1,500, compared to the $10,000 direct buy-in.
In poker, 'aggression factor' (AF) is a statistical measure that compares a player's aggressive actions (bets and raises) to passive ones (calls).
The 'Big One for One Drop' is a high-stakes charity poker tournament with buy-ins of $1 million, with a portion going to the One Drop Foundation.
Online poker revenue worldwide exceeds $5 billion annually.
In poker, a 'lag' (loose-aggressive) player enters many pots with a wide range and plays them aggressively.
The 'Fundamental Theorem of Poker,' coined by David Sklansky, states that every time you play differently from how you would if you could see opponents' cards, they gain.
A 'short stack' in tournament poker is a stack small enough that the player's options are limited primarily to shoving or folding.
The WSOP Asia-Pacific series was held in Melbourne, Australia, from 2013 to 2014 before being discontinued.
In poker, 'tanking' for too long is considered poor etiquette and some tournaments use shot clocks to address this.
Andy Beal, a billionaire banker, took on 'The Corporation'—a group of top poker pros—in a series of legendary heads-up games at the Bellagio.
The 'color up' process in tournaments involves exchanging smaller denomination chips for larger ones as blinds increase.
In poker, 'counterfeit' happens when a community card duplicates one of a player's pair cards, reducing their hand's value.
A 'monster' hand in poker is an exceptionally strong hand, such as a full house, four of a kind, or straight flush.
Pius Heinz won the 2011 WSOP Main Event as the first German champion.
In poker, 'c-betting frequency' is one of the most tracked statistics in player analysis, as it reveals how often a player follows up with a bet after raising pre-flop.
Johnny Moss won the first three WSOP Main Events (1970, 1971, and 1974), although the 1970 event was decided by vote rather than play.
A 'flip' in poker describes an all-in situation where both players have roughly 50/50 equity, like AK vs QQ.
In poker, 'bet sizing' is a critical skill—different situations call for different fractions of the pot.
The World Poker Tour Champions Club includes notable winners like Gus Hansen, Carlos Mortensen, and Antonio Esfandiari.
Nevada, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, and Michigan are among the states that have legalized regulated online poker.
In poker, 'backdoor' draws require hitting on both the turn and river to complete, giving them low probability but occasional strategic value.
A 'shootout' tournament requires a player to win their table before advancing to the next round.
In poker, 'ranges vs. hands' thinking means analyzing what an opponent could have (range) rather than trying to guess the exact hand.
The Super High Roller Bowl is an invitation-only tournament with buy-ins of $300,000 or more.
Ryan Riess won the 2013 WSOP Main Event at age 23, earning $8.36 million.
In poker, 'equity realization' describes how much of a hand's theoretical equity a player can actually capture based on position and skill.
Poker apps on smartphones have made it possible to play poker anytime and anywhere, contributing to the game's global growth.
A 'hero call' is calling a large bet with a marginal hand based on a strong read that the opponent is bluffing.
In poker, 'hand-reading' is the process of narrowing down an opponent's range based on their pre-flop and post-flop actions.
The 'big blind ante' format, which replaced individual antes, speeds up tournament play by having the big blind post the ante for the entire table.
Greg Merson won the 2012 WSOP Main Event and also earned the Player of the Year award.
In poker, 'adjusting to opponents' is a core skill—tight players require loose play to exploit, and vice versa.
Poker Night in America is a popular cash game show featuring both professional and celebrity players.
A 'card dead' stretch in poker is a prolonged period of receiving poor starting hands.
In poker, 'showdown value' describes a hand that is strong enough to win at showdown but not strong enough to bet for value.
The WSOP Paradise series launched in the Bahamas, adding a Caribbean stop to the WSOP circuit.
Phil Hellmuth has finished in the money at the WSOP Main Event more than a dozen times, a testament to his tournament longevity.
A 'cash out' or 'leave the table' in a cash game can happen at any time, as chips are directly convertible to money.
In poker, 'mental game' skills like emotional control, focus, and handling adversity are just as important as technical strategy.
The first legal online poker hand in the United States was dealt in Nevada on April 30, 2013.
A 'nitroll' is the frowned-upon act of slow-rolling with the absolute nuts, considered extremely poor etiquette.
In poker, the 'small ball' strategy, popularized by Daniel Negreanu, involves playing many pots with small bets and minimal risk.
Phil Ivey won his 10th WSOP bracelet in 2014, putting him third on the all-time list behind Phil Hellmuth and Johnny Chan.
A 'rock' is a very tight, conservative player who only enters pots with premium hands.
In poker, 'ICM pressure' at a final table can force players to fold hands they would normally play.
The 'poker triangle' represents the three pillars of the game: mathematics, psychology, and discipline.
Scott Blumstein won the 2017 WSOP Main Event, earning $8.15 million.
In poker, 'run it once' versus 'run it twice' affects variance but not expected value—it's purely a risk management choice.
The Super High Roller events at major poker festivals regularly feature pots exceeding $1 million.
A 'seat draw' in tournament poker randomly assigns players to specific table positions at the start of the event.
In poker, 'game selection' is the practice of choosing tables and games where you have the biggest edge over opponents.
The WSOP Circuit ring is a prestigious award for winning a WSOP Circuit event, a tier below the coveted gold bracelet.
John Cynn won the longest WSOP Main Event heads-up battle in 2018, lasting nearly 10 hours and 199 hands.
In poker, a 'maniac' is a hyper-aggressive player who raises and re-raises with a very wide range of hands.
The poker community has embraced charity events, with major charity tournaments raising millions for various causes.
A 'scare card' is a community card that could reasonably complete a strong draw or improve an opponent's hand.
In poker, 'blind defense' refers to the strategy of playing hands from the big blind to avoid losing too much to steals and raises.
Hevad 'Rain' Khan gained internet fame at the 2007 WSOP Main Event for his energetic celebrations at the table.
Chris Moneymaker's WSOP victory is often credited as the single most important event in poker history for growing the game's popularity.
In poker, 'hand equity' can change dramatically with each community card—a concept known as 'equity shift.'
The 'final table' in a tournament refers to the last remaining table, typically the final 9 or 10 players.
A 'calling range' is the set of hands with which a player will call rather than fold or raise.
The WSOP officially moved to Paris Las Vegas and Horseshoe Las Vegas (formerly Bally's) starting in 2022.
In Chinese Poker, players earn royalty bonuses for premium hands like a straight flush in the back or trips in the front.
Professional poker players often hire coaches and participate in study groups to continually improve their game.
A 'heads-up display' (HUD) overlays real-time statistics on online poker tables, showing opponent tendencies based on hand histories.
In poker, 'table dynamics' encompass the collective mood, aggression level, and playing styles at a specific table.
Bobby Baldwin won the 1978 WSOP Main Event and later became president of the Bellagio casino.
The minimum number of players for a poker game is two (heads-up), while a full ring cash game typically seats 9 or 10.
In poker, 'check behind' refers to checking when in position after the out-of-position player has already checked.
Poker's classification as a game of skill rather than pure chance has been upheld by multiple court rulings around the world.
A 'freezeout' format ensures every player starts with the same number of chips and there are no rebuys or add-ons.
Espen Jorstad from Norway won the 2022 WSOP Main Event, earning $10 million.
A 'wheel' in poker is the lowest possible straight: A-2-3-4-5, also called a 'bicycle.'
In Omaha, a player must use exactly two hole cards, unlike Hold'em where any combination of hole and board cards can be used.
The 'Poker Boom' of 2003-2006 saw online poker revenue grow from $82 million to over $2.4 billion annually.
A 'ring game' is another term for a cash game, where chips represent real money and players can join or leave at any time.
Dan Harrington's 'Harrington on Hold'em' book series is considered essential reading for tournament poker strategy.
In poker, 'pot geometry' describes how the pot grows across streets and how to plan bet sizes accordingly.
The 'Aussie Millions' held at Crown Melbourne is one of the Southern Hemisphere's most prestigious poker festivals.
A 'cold call' is calling a raise (or re-raise) without having previously put money in the pot on that betting round.
Online poker sites use random number generators (RNGs) certified by independent agencies to ensure fair card dealing.
In poker, 'three-betting light' means re-raising pre-flop with a hand that is not premium, often as a bluff or semi-bluff.
The 'Dead Man's Hand' legend says Wild Bill Hickok held black aces and eights when Jack McCall shot him from behind.
A 'button straddle' is a variation where the player on the dealer button posts a blind raise, gaining last action pre-flop.
Poker has more possible game states than chess, making it one of the most complex strategic games ever studied.
In 2017, the AI program Libratus defeated four top professional players in a 20-day heads-up No-Limit Hold'em challenge.
A 'wrap' in Omaha is a straight draw with more outs than a standard open-ended straight draw, sometimes having up to 20 outs.
Poker bankroll management suggests having at least 20-30 buy-ins for cash games and 50-100 buy-ins for tournament play.
The 'Broadway' cards in poker are the five highest-ranking cards: Ace, King, Queen, Jack, and Ten.
Pluribus, an AI developed by Carnegie Mellon and Facebook, defeated elite professionals in six-player No-Limit Hold'em in 2019.
In poker, 'nosebleed stakes' refer to the highest-limit games available, online or live, often with blinds of $200/$400 or higher.
A 'final table bubble' is the elimination spot just before the final table forms, often with significant pay jumps.
The WSOP added Europe and Asia-Pacific series to expand the brand globally beyond its Las Vegas origins.
In poker, 'card removal effects' (blockers) influence strategy—holding the A♠ when the board has three spades makes opponent flushes less likely.
Maria Ho is one of the most successful female tournament players, with over $4 million in career earnings and multiple deep WSOP Main Event runs.
